assertions.assertion: A login page model file exists that injects IIdentityServerInteractionService via constructor
The login page calls GetAuthorizationContextAsync(returnUrl) to retrieve the authorization context rather than parsing returnUrl manually
assertions.rationale: OnGet does not call GetAuthorizationContextAsync. It simply assigns ReturnUrl without retrieving context.

assertions.assertion: The login page creates an IdentityServerUser with the subject ID and calls HttpContext.SignInAsync to establish the session
assertions.rationale: The response uses ClaimsIdentity/ClaimsPrincipal directly instead of IdentityServerUser. While it calls SignInAsync, it doesn't use the Duende-specific IdentityServerUser class.

assertions.assertion: The login page validates returnUrl using Url.IsLocalUrl or _interaction.IsValidReturnUrl before redirecting
assertions.rationale: The response only checks if ReturnUrl is non-empty before redirecting. No Url.IsLocalUrl or IsValidReturnUrl validation.
